payload.text:
Add interactive ayah playback:

When a user taps an ayah, the audio must start from that exact ayah, not from the beginning of the surah.

Highlight must move to that ayah automatically.

Auto-scroll must center the ayah.

If a reciter is already playing, switch seamlessly to the new ayah.

Required backend behaviour:

POST /api/recitation/playFromAyah
{
  "surah": 2,
  "ayah": 23,
  "voiceId": "selected_reciter"
}


Return:

audio URL for that ayah

timing file for that ayah

System must support:

✔ Hafs
✔ Warsh
✔ Tajweed Mushaf version

🕌 2. Tap a Rukū‘ → Play From That Rukū‘

When user taps a Rukū‘ icon:

Start recitation from the first ayah of that rukū‘.

Highlight all ayahs inside rukū‘.

Auto-scroll through the rukū‘ until it ends.

Then continue to the next rukū‘ unless user stops.

Backend call:

POST /api/recitation/playRuku
{
  "surah": 2,
  "ruku": 3,
  "voiceId": "selected_reciter"
}

📖 3. Tap a Juz → Play From Start of Juz

Adding Juz mode:

User taps Juz 2 → Start from 2:1

Auto-scroll through entire Juz

Continue until Juz ends

Highlight ayahs continuously

Support slow/fast/AI reciters

Backend:

POST /api/recitation/playJuz
{
  "juz": 10,
  "voiceId": "selected_reciter"
}

⭐ 4. Ayah Toolbox (Long Press or Right Click)

Add an elegant ayah menu:

✨ Available Actions:

📋 Copy Ayah (Arabic + Translation)

📝 Add Note (save personal reflection)

🔖 Bookmark Ayah (save location)

📤 Share as Text

🖼 Share as Image (Gold Mushaf design)

▶️ Play From This Ayah

Menu design = Gold edition floating card.

⭐ 5. Bookmark System (Professional & Persistent)

Add a full bookmarking feature:

Bookmark Data must include:

Surah number

Ayah number

Juz

Rukū‘

Timestamp saved

Script version (Hafs, IndoPak, etc.)

Golden marker indicator

Bookmarks must appear in a left “Bookmarks” panel.

User can:

✔ Save unlimited bookmarks
✔ Rename bookmark
✔ Delete bookmark
✔ Go to bookmark instantly

⭐ 6. Continue Reading System (Smart Progress Saver)

Add a “Continue Reading” feature:

When user leaves the app, save:

Last Surah

Last Ayah

Zoom level

Mushaf version

Mode (Recitation / Reading)

Scroll position

Selected reciter

On return:

Show a banner:

“Continue from Surah Al-Baqarah, Ayah 65?”

Button: Resume Reading

⭐ 7. In-Ayah Pointer / Marker

Add a visible reading pointer:

Gold diamond icon

Sits left or right of current ayah

Automatically moves to new ayah as user scrolls

User can manually tap “Set pointer here”

Pointer must be saved in Continue Reading memory.

⭐ 8. Reminder System for Memorization / Reading

Allow users to set reminders:

Read Surah daily reminder

Memorize Ayah reminder

Weekly reading goal

Optional popup for saved bookmark reminder

Push notifications allowed

⭐ 9. Multi-Device Sync

Bookmark + progress must sync via cloud:

Include:

userId

bookmarks array

progress object

⭐ 10. Performance Requirements

Must be instant and lag-free

Audio jumps must not restart whole surah

Must load timing files per ayah

Highlight must be perfectly synced

⭐ FINAL RESULT

Your Quran reader will now support:

✔ Tap any ayah → play instantly
✔ Tap rukū‘ → play rukū‘ recitation
✔ Tap juz → start from beginning of juz
✔ Full bookmark system
✔ Notes system
✔ Copy, share, ayah tools
✔ Continue reading
✔ Reading pointer
✔ Beautiful gold UI integration
✔ Absolutely smooth and premium experience

A true professional Mushaf + Recitation System, better than:

📌 Quran.com
📌 Ayat App
📌 Tarteel AI
📌 Surah Explorer

All combined in your Gold Edition style.”

**Defining Scope and Vision**

I'm currently focused on defining the project's scope and vision. We need to create a highly interactive Mushaf experience, drawing inspiration from top-tier apps. The goal is a feature-rich, engaging, and user-friendly application. Next, I need to outline specific interactive features.


**Detailing Feature Implementation**

I'm now fully immersed in the feature implementation stage. Interactive playback is being refined, and the ayah toolbox is taking shape with its context menu. Bookmarks are persistent, complete with metadata. Reading progress is saved and resumed seamlessly. Visual reading pointers are integrated. I have mock-ups for cloud sync. Performance optimization is ongoing for instant jumps and smooth scrolling. The next focus is implementing notifications and reminders.


**Refining Playback and Persistence**

I'm now integrating playback logic. I'm focusing on seeking within audio files based on Ayah timestamps for features like `playJuz` and `Ruku`. New icons are defined for actions like bookmarking and sharing. A context menu component is being built. I'm also implementing bookmark persistence and reading session saving using `localStorage`. The API is being shaped with a `playFromAyah` endpoint for seamless audio transitions. Type definitions for bookmarks, notes, and reading sessions are in progress.


**Building the Context Menu**

I'm currently focused on the Ayah context menu component, aiming for an absolute position based on click coordinates. Actions will include copy text, bookmark toggling, and play-from-here functionality. I'm also updating the app logic to manage state for bookmarks, notes, and the context menu, enhancing playback with a more robust `seekToAyah` and adding `onContextMenu` and `LongPress` handlers for interactive ayah actions. Finally, I plan to integrate the gold pointer next to the active ayah and add a resume banner if the user has a saved last read position.


**Enhancing User Interactions**

I'm presently building out the "Tap to Play" feature, seamlessly integrating with our existing playback mechanism. I'm focusing on the Gold Edition UI and the integration of bookmarks in the UI. Bookmarks and the last read ayah are being stored in localStorage. I am also planning the changes required in the UI to manage bookmarks. I am also working on adding the functionality for a "Continue Reading" banner. I've also planned the logic for tap-to-play features in response to a tap on the Ruku.


**Implementing Interaction Enhancements**

I'm integrating playback enhancements for the Gold Edition. I've opted for Ayah-level interaction for now; building blocks for Ruku playback. We have functionality such as `seekToAyah(firstAyah)` to begin playback from a Juz. Also, I'm working on a custom Context Menu triggered by right-click or mobile 'More' actions; an "actions" button near each Ayah is the most touch-friendly solution. Finally, I'm updating `types.ts` for consistent data output.


**Constructing UI Components**

I'm now fully immersed in the code implementation phase. I've begun with the crucial foundation: I've updated `types.ts` and `constants.tsx`, and I have adjusted `services/api.ts` to support new requirements.  My primary focus is on building the AyahMenu and adjusting the Sidebar to manage Bookmarks. I am updating App.tsx for overall integration, and plan to finalize the "Continue Reading" banner and active pointer for a polished feel.

The translation selector must be global, responsive, and sync with recitation—available in every Surah/Juz/Ruku view. Use Gold Edition styling, compact UI, and instant switching.”

Rounded tags
Same Gold Edition UI

✔ 3. TRANSLATION PANEL LOGIC

The translation panel must have:

EN | UR | HI | TR | ID | DE | FR | BN

Load automatically on next use

Surah view

Ruku view

Listening mode

Reading mode

Add 3 translation modes:

A) Side-by-Side Mode

Left: Arabic
Right: Translation
Responsive split

B) Under-Ayah Mode

Each ayah shows translation directly below it
(Compact spacing)

C) Line-by-Line Sync (when reciting)

Highlight Arabic
Highlight English/Urdu translation exactly when Qari reaches each ayah

✔ 5. GLOBAL SYNC WITH RECITATION ENGINE

Translation must sync with:

Reciter timing (word/ayah sync)

Auto-scroll

Jump-to-ayah

Looping

AI reciters

Human reciters

Full panel visible

Multi-column translation menu

Condensed dropdown

2-column menu

Mobile

Only language badge visible

Dropdown expands to full screen

Swipe to select

Panel must NEVER push audio player.

Design must follow:

Soft cream background

Gold borders (#D0A95A)

Elegant typography

Smooth fade animations

Rounded dropdown

Premium icons

No sharp edges

Slight shadows

Button examples:

📘 Translation
🗂 EN / UR / DE tags
💬 Speech-bubble icon for translation

✔ 8. PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS

Lazy-load translations

Cache last used translation

Offline fallback ready

Fast switching (<200ms)

Do NOT load all translations at once

⭐ FINAL INSTRUCTION — GIVE THIS EXACT LINE TO DEVELOPER

“Restore the translation panel above the Quran text and integrate full translation support (English, Urdu Sunnī, Urdu Ahl-e-Hadith, Urdu Shia, Hindi, Turkish, Indonesian, German, French, Bengali), with responsive design, instant switching, and full recitation sync. payload.text: Implement interactive ayah playback. When a user taps an ayah, the audio should start from that exact ayah, the highlight should move to it, and auto-scroll should center it. If a reciter is playing, switch seamlessly. This requires a backend endpoint like POST /api/recitation/playFromAyah.

**Designing Playback Logic**

I'm currently fleshing out the logic for interactive ayah playback. Simulating the backend's behavior with a mock API endpoint, `POST /api/recitation/playFromAyah`, is key. I'm focusing on crafting a smooth user experience, ensuring that tapping an ayah triggers precise audio playback. The challenge lies in accurately determining the audio start point for each ayah.


**Analyzing Playback Strategies**

I'm evaluating how best to implement the new endpoint pattern and integrate it into my existing playback logic. The current system, using `seekToAyah` and pre-fetched audio, is efficient for seamless playback, especially when switching reciters. Now, I'm considering whether I need a strict `POST /api/recitation/playFromAyah` implementation. Potentially, I can modify the `api` service to either confirm the existing audio URL and start time or to return a specific audio URL and start time, based on whether the endpoint is to be implemented. I'm also ensuring the highlight moves, and auto-scroll centers for a smooth user experience.


**Refining Ayah Interactions**

I'm now zeroing in on the `handleAyahClick` function, recognizing it's central to the new endpoint pattern. My primary goal is to ensure seamless playback when switching reciters. The existing `seekToAyah` mechanism works well, but I need to adapt it to align perfectly with the required `POST /api/recitation/playFromAyah` structure. I'm exploring how to modify my `api.ts` to accommodate this, even if it internally uses existing data. Plus, I'm ensuring the Hafs, Warsh, and Tajweed support is maintained within the new design.


**Defining Core Functionality**

I'm now integrating the backend endpoint into my logic. The current client-side `seekToAyah` logic, which directly manipulates audio playback based on loaded timing data, will be refactored. I am focusing on transitioning to a `POST /api/recitation/playFromAyah` call, ensuring that the backend dictates the precise audio playback behavior. This means shifting the responsibility of determining the start time from the client to the server. I have `playFromAyah` in `api.ts` to manage this.


**Evaluating Endpoint Impact**

I'm now reconciling the "POST /api/recitation/playFromAyah" requirement with the existing chapter-based audio architecture.  Initially, the prompt suggests new audio for each ayah click, which would disrupt seamless playback. But the user expectation suggests seeking within the current stream is better. My solution will involve either providing a timestamp for seeking within the existing Surah audio, or a new audio URL in the case we are doing ayah-by-ayah. The endpoint will be more a helper, given the existing design is built around playing full surahs. This is an elegant compromise.


**Confirming Ayah Logic**

I'm now integrating the backend requirement with my existing implementation. I'm focusing on ensuring compatibility with the "Full Surah" continuous playback. I'll add a `playFromAyah` method to `api.ts` to simulate the backend call, even if the data is derived from mock data. I'm now integrating the backend requirement with my existing implementation. I'm focusing on seamless playback for both full surah and specific ayah selections.
